message:
fix(http): replace polling-based timeout with asupersync async timeout

The previous keep-alive timeout implementation used a poll_fn with manual
deadline checking and waker self-wakeup, which was a CPU-intensive busy-wait
pattern that didn't integrate properly with the async runtime's timer driver.

Changes:
- Add `current_time()` helper using OnceLock for lazy-initialized time base
- Replace custom poll_fn timeout with asupersync::time::timeout wrapper
- Use proper async timeout that integrates with the runtime's timer wheel
- Fix variable shadowing by renaming `timeout` param to `timeout_duration`

The asupersync timeout implementation properly registers with the timer
driver and only wakes when the timeout expires or data arrives, eliminating
unnecessary CPU cycles during idle keep-alive periods.

Also simplify PlaceholderHandler response:
- Use Response::with_status() convenience method instead of builder chain
- Keep 501 Not Implemented status for unimplemented route handlers

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
